metadata.scheme_name: Schemes for welfare of School Children belonging to BPL and BC(A) families: Monthly Stipend for BPL Students

raw_data.details: The scheme "Schemes for Welfare of School Children Belonging to BPL and BC(A) families: Monthly Stipend for BPL Students" was launched by the Government of Haryana during the financial year 2009-10. It aims to provide financial assistance to students from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to support their education. Under this scheme, a monthly stipend is provided to students from BPL families studying in classes 1 to 12 in Haryana Government schools.
raw_data.benefits: A. For Girl Students:Class 1 to 5: ₹150 per monthClass 6 to 8: ₹200 per monthClass 9 to 12: ₹300 per monthClass 11 to 12 (Science Students only): ₹400 per monthA. For Boy Students:Class 1 to 5: ₹75 per monthClass 6 to 8: ₹100 per monthClass 9 to 12: ₹150 per monthClass 11 to 12 (Science Students only): ₹200 per monthMode of Disbursement:Direct transfer to students' bank accounts. Frequency of Disbursement:Monthly basis. Expected Time of Disbursement:Funds to be transferred at the beginning of each quarter. Validity:Applicable for the duration of the academic session. Attendance criteria: Minimum 50% attendance required for next installment
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category. The applicant should be a student From Classes 1 to 12. The applicant should be enrolled in a Haryana Government school. The applicant should maintain at least 50% attendance to continue receiving benefits. The applicant should have an active bank account for stipend transfer.
raw_data.exclusions: The applicant who does not maintain 50% attendance will not receive the next instalment. The applicant not studying in a Haryana Government school is ineligible.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineThe applicant should contact their respective school authorities. For any queries, applicants can contact the school administration or visit the official website of the Education Department, Government of Haryana.
raw_data.documents_required: Proof of BPL Category (Certificate issued by a competent authority)School Enrollment CertificateAttendance Record (For stipend continuation)Bank Account Details (For Direct Benefit Transfer)Parent/Guardian Identity Proof (For account verification)Any other documents as required
raw_data.scheme_name: Schemes for welfare of School Children belonging to BPL and BC(A) families: Monthly Stipend for BPL Students

faqs.question: Who is eligible for Schemes for welfare of School Children belonging to BPL and BC(A) families: Monthly Stipend for BPL Students scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to BPL families who are enrolled in Haryana Government schools and meet the minimum attendance requirement can apply.

faqs.question: What is the amount of stipend provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The monthly stipend ranges from ₹75 to ₹400, varying by class level, gender, and stream.

faqs.question: How is the stipend distributed?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is transferred directly to the student’s bank account through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) system.

faqs.question: How frequently is the scholarship amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The stipend is disbursed on a monthly basis, with funds transferred at the beginning of each quarter.

faqs.question: What is the minimum attendance requirement to receive the stipend?
faqs.answer: Students must maintain at least 50% attendance in school to continue receiving the stipend.

faqs.question: How can a student apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students should visit their school, collect the application form, fill it out, and submit it along with the required documents to the school administration.

faqs.question: What happens if a student does not maintain 50% attendance?
faqs.answer: If a student’s attendance falls below 50%, the next instalment will not be released until attendance improves.

faqs.question: Can a student regain the scholarship if they improve their attendance?
faqs.answer: Yes, if a student regains 50% attendance in subsequent months, their pending and current instalments may be released.

faqs.question: Is this scholarship applicable to private school students?
faqs.answer: No, only students studying in Haryana Government schools are eligible for the scholarship.

faqs.question: Can students from other states apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only applicable to students enrolled in Haryana Government schools.

faqs.question: Is there a deadline to apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The school administration provides deadlines, and students should apply as early as possible for timely processing.

faqs.question: Who should be contacted for further queries regarding the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students should reach out to their school administration.
